evals.prompt: Is this research gap worth pursuing — applying LLMs to X?
evals.expected_behavior: Run sections 0-4 in order. Section 0 elicits and types the candidate Socratically (do NOT invent the topic). Section 1 uses adversarial-recall search and emits the .bib companion. Section 4 hands the worth-it verdict back to the researcher and advisor.

evals.prompt: Help me pick a thesis topic in agent-based flood modelling.
evals.expected_behavior: Elicit candidate breakthrough points Socratically; classify each Type A or B; do NOT decide which topic is best; assemble the 3-gate evidence and leave the choice to the researcher.

evals.prompt: Has someone already done this idea?
evals.expected_behavior: Treat as Gate 1 (Open?). Run adversarial-recall search, report a recall-confidence verdict as a headline, and never declare a gap 'open' from corpus-absence alone.
